LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HEIDI TIMKEN, LESLIE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER GONZALEZ AND TIMKEN JOHNSON, LLP'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CAUSE OF ACTION IMPLICATES CCP 425.16. PLAINTIFF IS THEREFORE OBLIGED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THE MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH MALICE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT NOTES THAT JUDGE KARNOW'S ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT STATED THAT THE MOTION WAS GRANTED BECAUSE THE FACTS WERE IN EQUIPOISE. THE CLOSE CALL IN GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT INDICATES THAT THE PARTIES HAD A LEGITIMATE DISPUTE AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY ESTABLISH A LACK OF PROBABLE CAUSE. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS ORDERED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS HEIDI TIMKEN, LESLIE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER GONZALEZ and TIMKEN JOHNSON, LLP's SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - No appearances are necessary and no further oral argument is required. The motion is granted. The malicious prosecution cause of action implicates CCP 425.16. Plaintiff is therefore obliged to present evidence to establish the malicious prosecution cause of action. Plaintiff fails to present sufficient evidence showing that the defendants acted with malice. In addition, the court notes that Judge Karnow's order granting summary judgment stated that the motion was granted because the facts were in equipoise. The close call in granting summary judgment indicates that the parties had a legitimate dispute and plaintiff does not sufficiently establish a lack of probable cause. The prevailing party is ordered to prepare a proposed order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =(302/EHG)
